_doc: Phase 5 Plan 05-06 — per-key ask-budget exhaustion canary. Sonnet asks about (ze, 0) three times across non-consecutive turns (1, 2, 5) — this DELIBERATELY does NOT trigger restrained mode (3 asks but spread over a >5-turn window so the rolling-window state machine sees only 2 in any 5-turn slice; the budget is keyed independently on (field, circuit) so it still trips at the 3rd attempt). The 1st and 2nd asks fire (counts 0→1, 1→2 per stage6-ask-budget Group 2 lock); the 3rd MUST short-circuit with answer_outcome='ask_budget_exhausted'. Per Plan 05-06 design: SMOKE-ONLY (gate_fixture:false) — the budget-exhausted outcome is itself benign for the aggregate gate (no activation), but the ask-count of 2 on this fixture would skew the head of the distribution; we keep it out so the gate population mirrors expected prod traffic.
_requirement: STA-06 + STB-04 — locks the per-key budget through the gate wrapper end-to-end. Pitfall 4: budget is incremented ONLY after a successful inner dispatch.
transcript_summary: 5-turn scenario. Turns 1, 2, 5 all involve the model asking 'which circuit was Ze?' — same (field=ze, circuit=null) key. Window between asks is wide enough not to trigger restrained mode but identical enough to exhaust per-key budget at the 3rd attempt.

input.question: Sorry — which Ze did you mean?
Could you clarify the Ze circuit again?
